evaluator_limitation.qfa_compatible_approximation: model.py implements a lagged close-to-close proxy using the latest completed daily bar's normalized overnight gap; the direct research harness evaluates the intended same-day open-to-close return from Alpaca OHLC bars.
evaluator_limitation.impact: qfa proxy smoke metrics are directionally useful for integration only and should not override the open-to-close research metrics.

commands.credentials: source /Users/moonk/.hermes/profiles/alphaaoi/secrets/alpaca.env; map ALPACA_KEY_ID to ALPACA_API_KEY; do not print secrets

db_used_during_run.db_retention_note: Temporary SQLite DB was removed after extracting compact qfa run metrics. No SQLite DB is retained in the model artifacts.

costs_slippage.cost_model: direct open-to-close harness subtracts 2 * per_side_cost_bps * gross / 10000 each event day; qfa CLI proxy has no cost parameter and is gross/pre-cost

orthogonality.reason: No retained parent AR-002 return stream or common factor library was available in the repo artifacts for correlation/orthogonality measurement; hypothesis is qualitatively distinct by horizon/open-to-close driver and liquid ETF universe.

divergent.title: Liquid ETF overnight gap continuation on macro trend days
divergent.hypothesis: Instead of extending the rejected gap-reversal mechanism, test whether large ETF overnight gaps continue when cross-asset risk indicators align, targeting macro-news trend continuation rather than opening-imbalance reversal.

decision.suggested_decision: Do not promote to production; keep as research reference only.
decision.rationale: The intended open-to-close strategy fails after realistic 5 bps per-side costs: Sharpe -0.813, annualized return -8.98%, total return -38.72%, and median random-window Sharpe -0.374. Gross open-to-close Sharpe is approximately flat, so the positive qfa close-to-close proxy is likely an engine/horizon artifact.

warnings: qfa daily close-to-close engine cannot exactly evaluate same-day open-to-close execution.
Open auction fills/slippage can be materially worse than the simple 5 bps per-side model.
No SQLite DB artifact is retained; rerun qfa with a temporary --db if run-level database inspection is needed.
